mission.title: etcd: go get error for 3.4.0
mission.description: go get error for 3.4.0. This issue affects 37+ users.

steps.title: Identify etcd troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your etcd deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n etcd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=etcd
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=etcd -n etcd --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review etcd configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant etcd configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n etcd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=etcd
kubectl get configmap -n etcd -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=etcd
```
```
go get go.etcd.io/etcd.4.0
go: finding golang.org/x/net master
go: finding go.etcd.io/etcd v3.4.0
go: finding go.etcd.io/etcd v3.4.0
go get go.etcd.io/etcd.4.0: go.etcd.io/etcd.4.0: invalid version: module contains a go.mod file, so major

steps.title: Apply the fix for go get error for 3.4.0
steps.description:
This is an attempt to resolve #11154 by completing the migration to Go
modules. This is a breaking change to anyone using Go older than 1.11.

On Go 1.11 or newer, with or without modules, the "go.etcd.io/etcd"
import path should still work.

Users that have already adopted modules are then
```yaml
git clone --branch v3.4.3 https://github.com/etcd-io/etcd.git
cd etcd
echo -n v0.0.0-
TZ=UTC git --no-pager show \
  --quiet \
  --abbrev=12 \
  --date='format-local:%Y%m%d%H%M%S' \
  --format="%cd-%h"
```

steps.title: Upgrade etcd to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade etcd:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade etcd etcd/etcd --namespace etcd
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n etcd
helm list -n etcd
```

steps.title: Confirm go get error for 3.4.0 is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=etcd -n etcd --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n etcd --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
This is an attempt to resolve #11154 by completing the migration to Go
modules. This is a breaking change to anyone using Go older than 1.11.

On Go 1.11 or newer, with or without modules, the "go.etcd.io/etcd"
import path should still work.

Users that have already adopted modules are then encouraged to use the
new, versioned import path "go.etcd.io/etcd/v3" in their projects.
codeSnippets:
git clone --branch v3.4.3 https://github.com/etcd-io/etcd.git
cd etcd
echo -n v0.0.0-
TZ=UTC git --no-pager show \
  --quiet \
  --abbrev=12 \
  --date='format-local:%Y%m%d%H%M%S' \
  --format="%cd-%h"

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with etcd installed or the issue environment reproducible.
